title: North America Clean Fuels Market Monthly Review: August 2025
content:
In the Clean Fuels Market, the United States Renewable Fuel Standard (US RFS) saw a decrease in RIN generation along with decrease in the number of RINs transacted in July. The California Low Carbon Fuel Standard (CA LCFS) saw a rise in the average Type 1 monthly credit price and a significant decline in monthly credit transactions in August. In the Washington Clean Fuel Standard (WA CFS), both average credit prices and trade volumes fell in July. On the other hand, the Oregon Clean Fuels Program (OR CFP) experienced a rise in average credit prices and trading activity in July. The Canada Clean Fuel Regulation (CFR) saw an increase in bid and offer prices for CC2 & CC3 credits in August. Lastly, in British Columbia Low Carbon Fuel Standard (BC LCFS), monthly trading volumes and credit prices both declined.

Under the U.S. Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS), the Environment Protection Agency (EPA) has decided to grant full exemptions to 63 small refinery exemption (SRE) petitions, granting partial exemptions to 77 petitions, denying 28 petitions, and determining 7 petitions to be ineligible. However, the EPA indicated it will propose a supplemental rule in the coming month to consider reallocating the associated RIN gallons and address the impact on the recently proposed 2026-2027 RFS volumes. In the EV space, California agencies delivered a report to Gov. Gavin Newsom outlining six priority actions to accelerate ZEV adoption with inputs from 1,000+ participants and 200 comments. The recommendations aim to expand clean vehicle adoption, safeguard consumers, and sustain Californiaâ€™s leadership despite federal rollbacks.
author: Radhika Khandelwal
